case_title: WILLIAM D. ROSS VS. STEPHEN R. CROW ET AL

docket_entries.description: PROOF OF SERVICE BY ELECTRONIC MAIL (TRANSACTION ID # 69186156) FILED BY DEFENDANT CROW, STEPHEN R.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEPHEN CROW'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. STEPHEN CROW'S DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER IS FILED. A DECLARATION MUST THEN BE FILED WHICH SPECIFIES WHETHER THE MEET AND CONFER WAS IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY MAY 5, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 69949862) FILED BY DEFENDANT CROW, STEPHEN R. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 2.0 DAYS

REMOVED FROM MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR JUN-18-2024 - ***CASE IS DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.*** NO APPEARANCE FOR SELF-REPRESENTED PLAINTIFF WILLIAM ROSS. SELAM GEZAHEGN, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. CASE WAS CALLED AT 9:32AM AND COURT WAS INFORMED THAT NOTICE WAS GIVEN BY THE CLERK AND SPOKE WITH BOTH PARTIES OVER THE PHONE YESTERDAY TO APPEAR TODAY BY 9:30AM FOR TRIAL ASSIGNMENT. MATTER WAS PASSED. CASE WAS RECALLED AT 9:40AM, NO APPEARANCE BY MR. ROSS. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS PRESENT. COURT WAS INFORMED THAT SOMEONE IS TRYING TO FILE DOCUMENTS AND THE CLERK INFORMED THAT PERSON TO COME UP IN DEPARTMENT 206 AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. MATTER WAS AGAIN PASSED. CASE WAS RECALLED AT 9:45AM, APPEARANCES BY SELAM GEZAHEGN, ESQ. AND DAVID SIMON, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT. NO APPEARANCE FOR SELF-REPRESENTED PLAINTIFF WILLIAM ROSS. MATTER WAS SET FOR TRIAL CALL AT 9:30AM TODAY AND THE FILING CLERK DOWNSTAIRS INFORMED THE COURT THAT SOMEONE WAS TRYING TO FILE SOMETHING DOWNSTAIRS AND THAT INDIVIDUAL WAS INSTRUCTED TO COME TO THIS DEPARTMENT, SEVEN OR EIGHT MINUTES AGO, AND WHOEVER THAT INDIVIDUAL WAS SHOULD HAVE BEEN HERE. DEFENDANT'S ORAL MOTION TO DISMISS. COURT WILL DISMISS THIS MATTER FOR PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO APPEAR AT TRIAL CALL. PURSUANT TO CCP 581(B)(3), THE COURT IS DISMISSING WITHOUT PREJUDICE, ON THE REQUEST OF DEFENDANT THAT THE ACTION WHEN NO PARTY APPEARS FOR TRIAL FOLLOWING 30 DAYS' NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL. TRIAL IS SET FOR YESTERDAY AND CONTINUED TO TODAY (6/18/24) AFTER IT WAS PLACED ON TELEPHONE STANDBY, SO THEN THERE'S SUFFICIENT NOTICE GIVEN TO PARTIES AND ALSO UNDER CCP 581(B)(5), BY THE COURT, WITHOUT PREJUDICE, WHEN EITHER PARTY FAILS TO APPEAR ON THE TRIAL AND THE OTHER PARTY APPEARS AND ASKS FOR DISMISSAL. THEREFORE, UNDER THE TWO SECTIONS OF CCP, MATTER IS DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-04-2023 Continued To Jul-05-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STEPHEN CROW'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Stephen Crow's demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. A declaration must then be filed which specifies whether the meet and confer was in person or by telephone. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the complaint is now due by May 5, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
